metadata.title: MCP Moira - Agent Workflow Engine with Control & Validation

conclusion.line1: You end up micromanaging every step.

whatIsMcp.title: Works With Your Tools. Changes Nothing.
whatIsMcp.description: MCP is a standard protocol already built into Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT, VS Code and other AI tools. It lets them connect to external services — like Moira.

setup.title: Setup: Add one line to your settings. That's it.

developmentFlow.title: Development Flow
developmentFlow.description: Full dev cycle: plan → code → tests → docs → git → notifications

research.title: Research
research.description: Structured research with verified sources and citations

codeReview.title: Code Review
codeReview.description: Security, performance, and quality checks with actionable feedback

testGeneration.title: Test Generation
testGeneration.description: Unit, integration, and E2E tests with coverage validation

developer.name: Developer
developer.problem: AI writes code but forgets tests and documentation
developer.solution: Development workflow — full cycle from plan to commit
developer.benefit: Tests, docs, git — nothing gets skipped

qaTester.name: QA/Tester
qaTester.problem: Tests written chaotically, coverage gaps everywhere
qaTester.solution: Test generation workflow — structured test creation
qaTester.benefit: Unit, integration, E2E with coverage validation

dataAnalyst.name: Data Analyst
dataAnalyst.solution: Research workflow — verified sources, citations required

productManager.name: Product Manager
productManager.problem: PRDs incomplete, missing edge cases and requirements
productManager.solution: PRD creation workflow — structured requirements gathering
productManager.benefit: Task breakdown with validation at each step

marketing.name: Marketing
marketing.solution: Content creation workflow — from outline to publication
marketing.benefit: Consistent tone, verified facts, proper formatting

createYourOwn.title: Need Something Custom?
createYourOwn.tagline: NO diagrams to draw. NO code to write. Just describe it.
createYourOwn.subtitle: Three ways to create your workflow:

template.title: Use a template
template.description: Start with a ready workflow, customize it for your needs
template.example: Example: "Take Code Review workflow, add security check"

describe.title: Describe in plain words
describe.description: Tell the agent what you need — it creates the workflow
describe.example: Example: "Create workflow for PR review with security checks"

scratch.title: Build from scratch
scratch.description: Full control over every step if you want it
scratch.example: Example: Define each step manually in the workflow editor

conversation.agentResponse: Done. Created 'Custom Code Review' workflow with 3 steps. Say 'start custom code review' to use it.

workflowPatterns.title: What You Can Build
workflowPatterns.subtitle: Workflows are built from simple patterns. Combine them as needed.

validation.title: Validation Loop (Critic Cycle)
validation.diagram: Create → Review → Issues? → Fix → Review again
validation.example: Agent reviews own work, repeats until perfect.

branching.title: Branching
branching.diagram: Check → Path A or Path B → Continue

schema.title: Schema Validation
schema.diagram: Agent provides data → Check format → Retry if wrong
schema.example: Agent can't proceed with wrong structure.

workflowPatterns.tagline: Mix and match patterns to build workflows that fit your needs.

builtWithMoira.title: Built With Moira
builtWithMoira.subtitle: Moira was built using Moira's own Development Workflow. Hundreds of iterations on real production code.

stepByStep.title: Step-by-step control
stepByStep.description: Agent completes each step before moving to the next. No skipping. No shortcuts.

builtInValidation.title: Built-in validation
builtInValidation.description: Each step has success criteria. Agent proves it's done before proceeding.

noForgottenTasks.title: No forgotten tasks
noForgottenTasks.description: Tests, docs, commits — nothing gets skipped. The workflow remembers even when agent doesn't.

consistentResults.title: Consistent results
consistentResults.description: Same workflow, same quality. Every time. No more "it worked yesterday".

hero.title: Make Your AI Agent Follow Instructions

subtitle.line2: Define the workflow once — your AI follows it step by step, every time.

hero.compatibility: Works with Claude Code, Cursor, ChatGPT, and any MCP client.

quickStart.title: Quick Start
quickStart.subtitle: Connect to MCP Moira in under 2 minutes

step1.text: Simply type in your AI chat:

step2.text: The agent will guide you through an interactive onboarding that explains Moira by demonstrating a workflow in action

steps.step3: You'll learn about available workflows and get started with your first real task!

claude-web.label: Claude Web
claude-web.content:
# On claude.ai (Pro/Max/Team/Enterprise):

# 1. Go to Settings → Connectors
# 2. Click "Add custom connector"
# 3. Enter:
#    Server URL: {{mcpUrl}}
# 4. Click "Connect"
# 5. Complete OAuth authentication in browser
# 6. Tools appear in chat

# Requires paid plan

chatgpt.label: ChatGPT
chatgpt.content:
# On chat.openai.com (Plus/Pro required):

# 1. Profile → Settings
# 2. Go to "Connectors" or "Integrations"
# 3. Click "Add connector"
# 4. Enter:
#    Name: MCP Moira
#    URL: {{mcpUrl}}
# 5. Complete OAuth authentication
# 6. Tools available in chat

# Free tier doesn't support MCP

claude-code.label: Claude Code
claude-code.authContent:
# After adding, authenticate within claude
/mcp
# → Select "moira"
# → Click "Authenticate"
# → Browser opens for OAuth

copilot-cli.label: Copilot CLI
copilot-cli.authContent:
# After saving the config:
# 1. Start a Copilot CLI session
# 2. Type /mcp
# 3. Select "moira" → Authenticate
# → Browser opens for OAuth
copilot-cli.alternativeContent:
# In Copilot CLI:
/mcp
# → Click "Add server"
# → Enter server URL
# → Complete OAuth

# Project-level config:
# .copilot/mcp-config.json (same format)

claude-desktop.label: Claude Desktop
claude-desktop.content:
# In Claude Desktop app:

# 1. Open Settings (⌘+,)
# 2. Go to "Connectors" tab
# 3. Click "Add custom connector"
# 4. Enter:
#    Server URL: {{mcpUrl}}
# 5. Click "Connect"
# 6. Browser opens → OAuth → Done

# No file editing

cursor.label: Cursor
cursor.ariaLabel: Install MCP Moira server in Cursor IDE
cursor.authContent:
# After clicking the button:
# 1. Cursor opens with install prompt
# 2. Click "Install" to add moira MCP server
# 3. Settings → MCP Servers → Find "moira"
# 4. Click "Authenticate" → Browser opens for OAuth

vscode.label: VS Code
vscode.ariaLabel: Install MCP Moira server in VS Code
vscode.authContent:
# After clicking the button:
# 1. VS Code opens with install prompt
# 2. Click "Install" to add moira MCP server
# 3. Settings → MCP Servers → Find "moira"
# 4. Click "Authenticate" → Browser opens for OAuth

perplexity.label: Perplexity
perplexity.content:
# Perplexity Mac App:

# 1. Install PerplexityXPC helper first:
#    Settings → Connectors → Install Helper
# 2. Click "Add Connector"
# 3. Enter:
#    Server Name: moira
#    Command: npx
#    Args: -y mcp-remote {{mcpUrl}}
# 4. Complete OAuth authentication
# 5. Ask Perplexity to use MCP Moira tools

# Paid plan recommended

continue.label: Continue
continue.description: VS Code extension: Open-source AI assistant
continue.content:
# Continue extension in VS Code:

# 1. Install Continue extension
# 2. Open config: Ctrl+Shift+P → "Continue: Open config"
# 3. Add to config.yaml:
#
# mcp:
#   servers:
#     moira:
#       url: {{mcpUrl}}
#       transport: http
#
# 4. Restart VS Code
# 5. Authenticate when prompted

zed.label: Zed
zed.description: Fast code editor with AI features

gemini-cli.label: Gemini CLI

tokenAuth.description: For CI/CD, Docker, or environments without a browser — use an API token instead of OAuth.
tokenAuth.steps:
1. Log in to Moira web UI → Settings → API Tokens
2. Create a token (starts with moira_)
3. Replace moira_YOUR_TOKEN below with your token

howItWorks.title: How It Works
howItWorks.subtitle: Four simple steps to controlled AI workflows

step1.title: Connect MCP Client
step1.description: Add MCP Moira server URL to your Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP-compatible tool. One-time setup.

step2.title: Start Workflow
step2.description: Call start with your workflow ID. MCP Moira returns the first step with instructions and schema.

step3.title: Execute Steps
step3.description: Agent completes each step and calls step. Response is validated against JSON Schema before proceeding.

step4.title: Get Results
step4.description: Workflow completes with all data validated and context preserved. Every step executed in order.

isFree.question: Is it free?
isFree.answer: Yes. MCP Moira is free to use. We may introduce premium features in the future, but the core functionality will remain free.

whatIsMcp.question: What is MCP?
whatIsMcp.answer: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard protocol for AI tools to connect to external services. It's already built into Cursor, Claude, ChatGPT, and other tools — no extra installation needed.

needToCode.question: Do I need to code workflows?
needToCode.answer: No. You can use ready-made workflows, describe what you need in plain words, or customize existing templates. No programming required.

betterThanPrompt.question: How is this better than a good prompt?
betterThanPrompt.answer: Prompts can be ignored or interpreted differently each time. Moira workflows have validation — the agent can't skip steps or produce wrong formats. Each step has success criteria that must be met before proceeding.

howWorksWithCursor.question: How does it work with my Cursor/Claude?
howWorksWithCursor.answer: Add one line to your settings (the MCP server URL). That's it. Your workflow stays the same — you just get more control over the agent's behavior.

notDeveloper.question: I'm not a developer. Can I use Moira?
notDeveloper.answer: Yes. Workflows exist for research, content creation, project management. No coding required.

createOwnWorkflow.question: Can I create my own workflow?
createOwnWorkflow.answer: Yes. Three ways: start from a template and customize, describe what you need and let the agent create it, or build from scratch with full control over each step.

setupTime.question: How long does setup take?
setupTime.answer: About 2 minutes. Add the server URL, authenticate once, and you're ready. No complex configuration or installation.

agentMistake.question: What if the agent makes a mistake?
agentMistake.answer: Two layers of protection: Schema validation ensures correct format, and Self-Review makes the agent check its own work. If issues are found, it fixes them before proceeding.

metadata.title: Developers - MCP Moira | Agent Workflow Engine

hero.title: Agent workflow engine for AI agents
hero.subtitle: Define multi-step workflows with validation at every step. Your AI agent executes consistently, every time.
hero.anchoring: Think of it as CI/CD for AI agent tasks.

forgottenContext.title: Lost Context
forgottenContext.description: Long prompts that the agent forgets halfway through. Complex tasks break into pieces that never reconnect.

noValidation.title: No Result Validation
noValidation.description: Agent says "done" but the code doesn't compile. No schema checks, no proof of completion.

hallucinations.title: Hallucinated Results
hallucinations.description: Agent invents files, functions, and test results that don't exist. Confident but wrong.

incomplete.title: 80% Done, 20% Forgotten
incomplete.description: Tests skipped. Documentation missing. Error handling ignored. The last mile never gets finished.

reExplaining.title: Re-explaining Every Time
reExplaining.description: Same process, different session. You explain the workflow from scratch because nothing persists.

noControl.title: No Execution Control
noControl.description: Agent skips steps, jumps ahead, takes shortcuts. No way to enforce a specific execution order.

connect.title: Connect
connect.description: Add Moira as an MCP server to your AI agent. One config line in Claude Code, Cursor, or any MCP client.

start.title: Start a Workflow
start.description: Choose a workflow — built-in or custom. The engine loads the execution graph and begins step-by-step processing.

execute.title: Agent Executes
execute.description: Each step has a clear directive and completion criteria. The agent works through the graph — validated, structured, complete.

autonomy.title: Agent Does the Work
autonomy.description: From planning to self-review. Define the task — get a working result. The workflow guides the agent through every phase: requirements → plan → implementation → tests → quality → review.

validation.title: Hallucination Protection
validation.description: JSON Schema validation at every step. The agent cannot skip ahead — each response must match the expected structure. Completion conditions define what "done" actually means.

codeQuality.title: Enforced Code Quality
codeQuality.description: Mandatory code quality checks and agent self-validation. Bounded fix loops — if quality doesn't meet standards, the agent retries (up to 3 attempts), then escalates to you.

testing.title: Automated Test Validation
testing.description: Test execution, failure analysis, and fix strategies — all inside the workflow. The agent doesn't just write code — it verifies that the code works.

documentation.title: Docs and Tests Stay Current
documentation.description: If your project has documentation and tests, the workflow includes their updates. The completion phase updates docs and generates a final report.

gitWorkflow.title: Branch Isolation Built In
gitWorkflow.description: Git workflow is part of the process — commits, PR creation, branch management. Everything is isolated from master until reviewed and approved.

notifications.title: Telegram When You're Needed
notifications.description: You don't sit in front of a screen. When the workflow needs your input — approve a plan, review code, resolve an issue — you get a Telegram notification.

workflowShowcase.title: Ready-Made Development Workflows
workflowShowcase.subtitle: Battle-tested workflows that cover the full software development lifecycle. Start using them today or customize to your needs.

full.label: SDF Full
full.description: Complete development workflow for features and complex tasks. Covers the entire lifecycle from requirements to deployment.

requirements.name: Requirements
requirements.description: Gather requirements, study project, define task scope

planning.name: Planning
planning.description: Create plan + agent review + fix cycle (max 3 iterations)

approval.name: User Approval
approval.description: Review and approve the plan before implementation

testing.name: Testing
testing.description: Run tests → analyze failures → fix strategy

quality.name: Code Quality
quality.description: Quality check + self-validation with bounded fix loops

review.name: User Review
review.description: Final review by developer with approval/rejection

lite.label: SDF Lite
lite.description: Streamlined workflow for quick tasks and smaller changes. Same quality guarantees, fewer ceremony steps.

connect.title: Connect MCP Server
connect.description: Add Moira as an MCP server in your AI client. One URL, OAuth authentication.

start.title: Start a Workflow
start.description: Tell your AI agent to start any workflow. Moira handles the rest.
start.code:
# In any MCP-compatible AI client:
> Start the software development workflow
  for issue #42

# Agent calls:
mcp__moira__start({
  workflowId: "moira/software-development-flow",
  parentExecutionId: "none"
})

execute.title: Follow the Workflow
execute.description: Agent receives step-by-step directives with success criteria. Each step validated before proceeding.

customWorkflows.title: Build Your Own Workflows
customWorkflows.subtitle: Ready-made workflows are the starting point. Create custom workflows for your team's specific processes.

webEditor.title: Visual Web Editor
webEditor.description: Create and edit workflows through the web interface. Node-graph editor with real-time validation.

jsonFirst.title: JSON-first Design
jsonFirst.description: Workflows are JSON files. Version control, code review, CI/CD — standard developer workflow applies.

templateVars.title: Template Variables
templateVars.description: Use {{variable}} syntax in directives and conditions. Context flows between steps automatically.

schemaValidation.title: Schema Validation
schemaValidation.description: JSON Schema on every step input/output. Agent responses validated before workflow proceeds.

featureDev.title: Automated Feature Development
featureDev.description: Full cycle: issue analysis → planning → implementation → testing → code review → commit. With quality gates at each phase.

codeReview.title: Structured Code Review
codeReview.description: Automated review with BLOCKING/MAJOR/MINOR classification. Consistent criteria across all PRs, no reviewer fatigue.

bugFix.title: Bug Fixing with Root Cause Analysis
bugFix.description: Reproduce → diagnose root cause → implement fix → verify no regression. Prevents band-aid fixes.

documentation.title: Documentation Generation
documentation.description: Extract API docs from code, generate guides from usage patterns, keep docs in sync with implementation.

research.title: Research with Iterative Improvement
research.description: Source verification, cross-referencing, quality scoring. Multiple iterations until research meets quality threshold.

t1.name: Alex K.
t1.text: Moira turned our chaotic prompt engineering into repeatable workflows. Code review consistency went from random to reliable.

t2.name: Sarah M.
t2.text: The JSON-first approach means I can version control my workflows. Schema validation catches agent mistakes before they reach production.

t3.name: Chen W.
t3.text: Three steps to get started, and it actually works. No sign-up wall, no sales call. Just MCP connect and go.

finalCta.title: Ready to Build?
finalCta.subtitle: Stop micromanaging your AI agent. Define the workflow once, execute it consistently every time.
